div1.entryFree:
Hem, a sort of voluntary half cough, used 1) by way of encouragement: cry h., when he should groan, Ado V, 1, 16. if I could cry h. and have him, As I, 3, 19.*when you breathe in your watering, they cry h. and bid you play it off, H4A II, 4, 18. how do you now? Better than I was, h.! Why, that's well said, H4B II, 4, 33. our watchword was h. boys, III, 2, 232. there's a man niece, h. Troil. I, 2, 248.
2) to give a warning: cough or cry h., if any body come, Oth. IV, 2, 29.
